issue_title: SAML2 Client Signing Keys Config does not accept PEM import
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

When I want to create a new SAML2 client in Keycloak and I want to require client signatures, I can click Regenerate and receive a private key. The conceptual problem is that
a) keycloak should never know the private key
b) I don't want to transfer the private key to my client.
Private keys are private. They should be generated on the client and remain on the client. What I actually want to do is to import the client's public certificate as trusted.
This behaviour is broken again (cf. https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/36708).

When trying to work around the issue, I tried to "turn it off and on again":

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c5087f23-8af8-4e72-8963-582e1b4a4055)

When you turn "Client signature required" back on, a dialog appears, letting you choose to either generate a client cert on KC or to import an existing one:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/55d8a339-3ac3-41d8-b8f4-927754134d51)

When choosing to import a client cert in this dialog, I am again forced to upload the private key to keycloak, as "Import Certificate PEM" is missing from the drop down:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/21cf2c2c-494e-4c20-8d70-0dcba8e02c63)

This relates to an older issue: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/17514

### Version

26.1.0

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

I can import a PEM certificate (only the public part) as SAML Client Certificate

### Actual behavior

I have to choose from JKS, PKCS12, BCFKS, which are all container formats which include the private key.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create SAML Client
2. Go to "Keys"
3. Deactivate "Client signature required"
4. Reactivate "Client signature required"
5. Choose "Import" Radio Button
6. Be disappointed, that PEM is missing from the Archive Formats :)

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
added pem to saml dialog (#37206)

fixes: #36709

Signed-off-by: Erik Jan de Wit <erikjan.dewit@gmail.com>

issue_title: invalid_request when authenticating using PAR (Pushed Authorization Request) while Kerberos is enabled
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

When a client uses Pushed Authorization Request to authenticate a user while the client in keycloak is set to use an authorization flow with kerberos enabled, it will cause an "Invalid Request" for the user. In the logs the following two messages appear:

```
2025-01-21 16:44:35,503 WARN  [org.keycloak.services] (executor-thread-16) KC-SERVICES0097: Invalid request: java.lang.RuntimeException: PAR not found. not issued or used multiple times.
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.par.endpoints.request.AuthzEndpointParParser.<init>(AuthzEndpointParParser.java:64)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.request.AuthorizationEndpointRequestParserProcessor.parseRequest(AuthorizationEndpointRequestParserProcessor.java:92)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.AuthorizationEndpoint.process(AuthorizationEndpoint.java:144)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.AuthorizationEndpoint.buildGet(AuthorizationEndpoint.java:117)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.AuthorizationEndpoint$quarkusrestinvoker$buildGet_4b690b27439f19dd29733dc5fd4004f24de0adb6.invoke(Unknown Source)
	at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.server.handlers.InvocationHandler.handle(InvocationHandler.java:29)
	at io.quarkus.resteasy.reactive.server.runtime.QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.invokeHandler(QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.java:141)
	at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.common.core.AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.run(AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.java:147)
	at io.quarkus.vertx.core.runtime.VertxCoreRecorder$14.runWith(VertxCoreRecorder.java:635)
	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Task.doRunWith(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:2516)
	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Task.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:2495)
	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$ThreadBody.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:1521)
	at org.jboss.threads.DelegatingRunnable.run(DelegatingRunnable.java:11)
	at org.jboss.threads.ThreadLocalResettingRunnable.run(ThreadLocalResettingRunnable.java:11)
	at io.netty.util.concurrent.FastThreadLocalRunnable.run(FastThreadLocalRunnable.java:30)
	at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:1583)

2025-01-21 16:44:35,506 WARN  [org.keycloak.events] (executor-thread-16) type="LOGIN_ERROR", realmId="dahag-internal", realmName="dahag-internal", clientId="authentication-dummy", userId="null", ipAddress="10.0.21.46", error="invalid_request"
```

It seems like the issue is using both in conjunction because it will work if either the client doesn't use PAR or when Kerberos is disabled on the authentication flow. The current workaround for us was to disable PAR in our clients (we upgraded from net8 to net9 and the new aspnetcore defaults to using PAR when possible which is why this suddenly started happening for us)

### Version

26.1.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Authentication should continue as usual

### Actual behavior

User gets served an error page saying "Invalid Request"

### How to Reproduce?

On an fresh keycloak:  
- Configure an LDAP user federation with kerberos enabled (Doesn't matter if the ldap nor kerberos is working. Configuring them in a completely broken state yields the exact same logs and errors compared to an actual working federation and kerberos)
- do an authentication flow using PAR

### Anything else?

_No response_

redirectToAuthentication if the request uses PAR to not lose the single object after a refresh
Closes #36716

issue_title: Logging errors on DB transaction retries
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

When retrying a transaction, Keycloak logs an error. This due to the JpaTransactionWrapper violating the either-lor-or-throw best practice, as it does both log AND throw. 

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/35047#issuecomment-2608455509

```
2025-01-22 14:25:47,058 ERROR [org.keycloak.transaction.JtaTransactionWrapper] (org.keycloak.models.sessions.infinispan.changes.PersistentSessionsWorker$BatchWorker) Exception during transaction operation. Request context: Non-HTTP task, Transaction: TransactionImple < ac, BasicAction: 0:ffff0afe0012:acd9:678a1500:12386 status: ActionStatus.ABORTED >
```

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

An exception should be either thrown or logged, but not both. Logging additional information in debug mode would be ok

### Actual behavior

It is both logged as an error and thrown. 

### How to Reproduce?

See log

### Anything else?

I'll prepare a PR

issue_title: External (IDP) token-exchange is possible even for clients needing user consents
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

token-exchange

### Describe the bug

When client needs user consents ("Consent required" = true) then token-exchange should not be possible.
This worked correctly still in previous version 26.0.7.

### Version

26.1.0

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When "Consents required" = true for specific client, then external token-exchange should not be possible.

### Actual behavior

Token-exchange is possible even for clients which require user-consents.

### How to Reproduce?

Enabled features:
- token-exchange
- admin-fine-grained-authz 

Create Identity-Provider:
- alias = test-idp
- issuer = test-idp
- userinfo = some-userinfo

Create client scope:
- name = TEST_SCOPE
 
Create client:
- client_id = client-abc
- consent-required = true
- add scope TEST_SCOPE as default
- allow client to use test-idp token-exchange

Create external token that passes userinfo specified in "test-idp"

Call:
POST {{keycloak}}/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/token
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded

grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:token-exchange
&client_id=client-abc
&client_secret={{client-secret}}
&subject_issuer=test-idp
&subject_token={{external-token}}

Response:
Actual:
200 with generated access-token containing scope "TEST_SCOPE"

Expected:
400 with error:
{"error":"invalid_client","error_description":"Client requires user consent"}


### Anything else?

_No response_

Check client consents when audience is for the same client

issue_title: Conflict when Keycloak uses an OpenShift cluster ingress certificate
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

operator

### Describe the bug

If a Keycloak instance is deployed using the OpenShift cluster ingress certificate , *.app.<cluster-domain> , when a client (browser) establishes a connection with Keycloak, the requests to other applications deployed on the same OpenShift cluster may be received by Keycloak instead of the requests being received by the application.

The Keycloak connection is reused and the request is received by Keycloak.

Workaround

Use reencrypt route for ingress controller

A reencrypt route fixes the issue because for re-encrypt routes, the Ingress Controller negotiates its connection to the application independently of the connection from the client.

route creation must be disabled on Keycloak CR , setting .spec.ingress.enabled: false

~~~
apiVersion: k8s.keycloak.org/v2alpha1
kind: Keycloak
metadata:
name: keycloak1
spec:
instances: 1
db:
vendor: postgres
host: postgres-db-w
usernameSecret:
name: keycloak-db-secret
key: username
passwordSecret:
name: keycloak-db-secret
key: password
http:
tlsSecret: tls-secret-wildcard
hostname:
hostname: keycloak1.apps.<domain>
ingress:
enabled: false
~~~



### Version

24

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Requests should go to the appropriate application, which may not be Keycloak.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak receives requests that are intended for other applications

### How to Reproduce?

Deploy a Keycloak instance using the OpenShift cluster ingress certificate , *.app.<cluster-domain>

### Anything else?

_No response_

Openshift conflict

Closes #36745

issue_title: Addition of crl cache is a breaking change
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

infinispan

### Describe the bug

As part of this PR: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/36294 a new local Infinispan cache with name `crl` was introduced. This change is a breaking change for users using a custom XML file as the cache will not be created and Keycloak will refuse to start with an error: `ISPN000436: Cache 'crl' has been requested, but no matching cache configuration exists` . 

This needs to be fixed before Keycloak 26.2 is released as we can't introduce breaking changes in minor releases.

This can be fixed by adding a check in Java whether the cache exists in XML and if it is not there [print warning and] create it. This should be only a temporary solution and as part of the next major release it should be removed and migration guide should contain steps how to migrate.

 

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

There are no breaking changes in minor release

### Actual behavior

addition of an Infinispan cache is a breaking change for users using custom xml configuration

### How to Reproduce?

Run Keycloak with xml file that does not contain crl cache definition in xml file:
```
        <local-cache name="crl" simple-cache="true">
            <encoding>
                <key media-type="application/x-java-object"/>
                <value media-type="application/x-java-object"/>
            </encoding>
            <expiration lifespan="-1"/>
            <memory max-count="1000"/>
        </local-cache>
```

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Add default configuration for cache 'crl'

Fixes #36752

issue_title: Seaching users in the user selector will not show the username for users already selected
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

In pages using the `UserSelect` component to allow selecting users based on a search, there is a bug when you search for an user already selected and select it from the result list.

The current behavior of the component is to empty the username of the selected user once you search for the same user and select it.

In addition to that,  the component should not show in the search results the users already selected.

### Version

26.0.8

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Searching for users when using the `UserSelect` component should keep the existing users and their usernames and do not allow selecting the same user again.

### Actual behavior

Searching for users when using the `UserSelect` component removes the username for  existing users after selecting the same user and the search result does not filter the selected users.

### How to Reproduce?

* Create a client and enable authorization
* Navigate to the `Authorization` and then the `Policies` tab
* Create a `User Policy` by adding a user to it
* Go back to the policies listing page and click on the policy previously created
* In the `Users` field, type the username of the user already associated with the policy

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant Issues: Token Issued After Authorization Denial and Browser Back
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

When using Keycloak's OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant, if a user denies authorization and then navigates back in the browser to authorize again, an access token is still issued despite the initial denial. This behavior contradicts the expected security flow, potentially ignoring the user's intent to deny authorization.

### Version

26.1.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When a user denies authorization using the OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant, navigating back in the browser and attempting to authorize again should not result in an access token being issued. The denial state should be correctly maintained, and no token should be issued.

### Actual behavior

After a user denies authorization, navigating back in the browser and authorizing again results in an access token being issued despite the initial denial. This allows tokens to be issued unintentionally based on the user's denial.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Configure a client application to use the OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant flow in Keycloak.
2. Initiate an authorization request from the device, opening the authorization screen in the browser.
3. On the authorization screen, select "Deny" to refuse authorization.
4. Click the browser's "Back" button to return to the authorization screen.
5. Select "Allow" to authorize again.
6. Observe that an access token is issued despite the initial denial.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Avoid token issuance after denied consent in the Device Grant flow

Closes #36811

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Graziano <g.graziano94@gmail.com>

issue_title: NullPointerException when registering a oid4vc CredentialBuilder provider component
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oid4vc

### Describe the bug

Hello, I'm trying OID4VC experimental feature and noticed that the `credential_configurations_supported` field returned by the `.well-known/openid-credential-issuer` endpoint is empty. After digging a bit into the code I understood that I have to activate a CredentialBuilder provider.

When calling the `POST /admin/realms/master/components` to activate the `jwt_vc` provider I get a 500 error. 

### Version

nightly

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

I expect that the API to register the provider component and completes successfully.

```
curl -X POST "http://localhost:9081/admin/realms/master/components"   
-H "Authorization: Bearer $ACCESS_TOKEN"   
-H "Content-Type: application/json"   -d '{
    "name": "vctest",
    "providerId": "jwt_vc",
    "providerType": "org.keycloak.protocol.oid4vc.issuance.credentialbuilder.CredentialBuilder"
  }'
```

### Actual behavior

The abovementioned API call returns an error:

```json
{"error":"unknown_error","error_description":"For more on this error consult the server log."}
```

### How to Reproduce?

Launch a keycloak instance with the oid4vc-vci feature enabled.

Get an access token 
```
export ACCESS_TOKEN=$(curl -X POST \                                                                                                                            "http://localhost:8080/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/token" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
  -d "username=admin" \
  -d "password=admin" \
  -d "grant_type=password" \
  -d "client_id=admin-cli" | jq -r .access_token)
```

Register the provider:

```
curl -X POST "http://localhost:8080/admin/realms/master/components" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $ACCESS_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "my-custom-provider",
    "providerId": "jwt_vc",
    "providerType": "org.keycloak.protocol.oid4vc.issuance.credentialbuilder.CredentialBuilder"
  }'
```



### Anything else?

The error happens for all the providerIds of `CredentialBuilder`.

It seems that the reason is that the `getConfigProperties` of the `JwtCredentialBuilderFactory`, `LDCredentialBuilderFactory`, `SdJwtCredentialBuilderFactory` classes return null instead of an empty list.
commit_message:
Return empty configProperties for CredentialBuilderFactory implementations

Closes #36826

Signed-off-by: Enrico Costanzi <enrico.costanzi@staff.aruba.it>

issue_title: Documentation about ImportSynchronization mentions wrong interface UserStorageProvider
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

The JavaDoc for `org.keycloak.storage.user.ImportSynchronization` mentions that the interface should be implemented by any class that is implementing the `org.keycloak.storage.UserStorageProvider`. 
See here: 
- https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/5ab1bd2036831baea85626f17a54d462cede1658/model/storage/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/user/ImportSynchronization.java#L27

But the `org.keycloak.storage.managers.UserStorageSyncManager` is expecting the interface to be present at an implementation of a `org.keycloak.storage.UserStorageProviderFactory`. 
See here:
- https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/5ab1bd2036831baea85626f17a54d462cede1658/model/storage/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/user/ImportSynchronization.java#L27
- https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/5ab1bd2036831baea85626f17a54d462cede1658/model/storage-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/managers/UserStorageSyncManager.java#L88

If the observation is correct, I can create an PR updating the JavaDoc of the `org.keycloak.storage.user.ImportSynchronization` and any mentionings in relation to the `org.keycloak.storage.UserStorageProvider`.

### Version

26.1.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Have the JavaDoc explain that `org.keycloak.storage.user.ImportSynchronization` needs to be implemented at an `org.keycloak.storage.UserStorageProviderFactory`.

### Actual behavior

The JavaDoc for `org.keycloak.storage.user.ImportSynchronization` wrongfully refers to the `org.keycloak.storage.UserStorageProvider`. 

### How to Reproduce?

Compare the JavaDoc with the actual implementation in the `org.keycloak.storage.managers.UserStorageSyncManager`.

Also, if the `org.keycloak.storage.user.ImportSynchronization` is implemented at an `org.keycloak.storage.UserStorageProvider` it is not possible to synchronize any users through the admin console.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
docs: update JavaDoc for ImportSynchronization

The JavaDoc for ImportSynchronization was wrongfully referencing the
UserStorageProvider instead of the UserStorageProviderFactory.

Closes #36834

Signed-off-by: arthenice <wistful.arthenice@gmail.com>

issue_title: Update FGAP v2 to not grant permissions of all users when permission is granted only for a single user
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/fine-grained-permissions

### Describe the bug

When granting permissions to `manage` a single user/resource all users/resources are granted when calling the `org.keycloak.services.resources.admin.permissions.UserPermissions#canView()` method.

Instead, we should check if the permission actually granted maps to the generic resource (`Users`) because this method is only about checking "global" permissions based on the generic resource type.

The same applies for other methods where there is no specific resource object as a parameter.

### Version

999.0.0-SNAPSHOT

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Only grant access to the generic resource (e.g.: `Users`) if the permission granted was for that resource.

### Actual behavior

Access is granted to the generic resource (e.g.: `Users`) even though the permission granted was for a specific resource.

### How to Reproduce?

* Create two users
* Create a permission that only allow access to a specific user and resource
* Check if the user not associated with the permission is not shown when listing users

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Update FGAP v2 to not grant permissions of all users when permission is granted only for a single user 

Closes #36838

issue_title: Comboxes do not display selected option after reset

issue_title: Login with x-forwarded-for: IP address in user login event is null
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/cli

### Describe the bug

Since Keycloak version 26.0.6, the user login events do no longer show an IP address if the user logged in with x-forwarded-for.

### Version

26.1.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Login event shows the IP address from the x-forwarded-for header.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d477b1e0-04b6-49a5-8a37-206c5a0fae55)

### Actual behavior

IP address in the login event is null.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2d1620fb-b9af-4cbc-8805-4ada19a2c1db)

### How to Reproduce?

Requires a keycloak instance with https enabled.

1.) Build docker image: `docker build . -t my_keycloak_image:26.1.0`

Dockerfile:
```
FROM quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:26.1.0  as builder

ENV KC_HEALTH_ENABLED=false
ENV KC_METRICS_ENABLED=false
ENV KC_FEATURES=token-exchange

FROM quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:26.1.0
COPY --from=builder --chown=1000:1000 /opt/keycloak/ /opt/keycloak/
WORKDIR /opt/keycloak

RUN keytool -genkeypair -storepass password -storetype PKCS12 -keyalg RSA -keysize 2048 -dname "CN=<my_server>" -alias <my_server_alias> -ext "SAN:c=DNS:<my_server>,IP:<my_server_ip>" -keystore conf/server.keystore

ENTRYPOINT ["/opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh", "start"]
```

2.) Run docker image

`docker run -d -p 8443:8443 -e KC_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_USERNAME=admin -e KC_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin -e KC_PROXY_HEADERS=xforwarded -e KC_PROXY_ADDRESS_FORWARDING=true -e KC_HOSTNAME=<my_server> my_keycloak_image:26.1.0`

3.) Login at https://<my_server>:8443 and enable user events in the realm settings

4.) Use Postman or the like to obtain an access token:

POST https://<my_server>:8443/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/token
Body (x-www-form-urlencoded): grant_type=password, username=admin, password=admin, client_id=admin-cli
Header: x-forwarded-for=xxx.xxx.xxx.1234

5.) Make a request with the obtained access token:

GET https://<my_server>:8443/admin/realms
Authorization: Bearer, token

6.) Check the user events in the admin console

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: AuthenticationFlowContext.getRefreshUrl(true) - adds auth_session_id query param in an old non-supported format
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

When using AuthenticationFlowContext.getRefreshUrl(true) - "auth_session_id" query param is added to the result URL in an old, non-encoded format. 

    public URI getRefreshUrl(boolean authSessionIdParam) {
        UriBuilder uriBuilder = LoginActionsService.loginActionsBaseUrl(getUriInfo())
                .path(AuthenticationProcessor.this.flowPath)
                .queryParam(Constants.CLIENT_ID, getAuthenticationSession().getClient().getClientId())
                .queryParam(Constants.TAB_ID, getAuthenticationSession().getTabId())
                .queryParam(Constants.CLIENT_DATA, getClientData());
        if (authSessionIdParam) {
            uriBuilder.queryParam(LoginActionsService.AUTH_SESSION_ID, getAuthenticationSession().getParentSession().getId());
        }
        return uriBuilder
                .build(getRealm().getName());
    }

This results in an error later in the flow, when AuthenticationSessionManager.getAuthenticationSessionByEncodedIdAndClient is called.

### Version

26.1.0

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

"auth_session_id" query param should be added to the query in the new format introduced in 26.1.0 - encoded and signed.

### Actual behavior

"auth_session_id" query param is added as plain UUID

### How to Reproduce?

Use getRefreshUrl(true) of AuthenticationFlowContext in custom SPI, ie. 

`String loginRefreshUrl = context.getRefreshUrl(false).toString();`

Result will contain auth_session_id query param as UUID, ie.  "auth_session_id=8edf08a0-f15b-43b4-9789-b01df4c5c680"


### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Outdated documentation about how to use reCAPTCHA in development with localhost
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

docs

### Describe the bug

This seems to be a bug in keycloak`s docs, and they seem to be oudated - see [here](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/3232904/using-recaptcha-on-localhost)

If you Keycloak on localhost for development purposes, you can no longer use the recaptcha by default. Instead you need to add it to your reCAPTCHA configuration. See https://developers.google.com/recaptcha/docs/faq#localhost_support for more details: 

> I'm getting an error "Localhost is not in the list of supported domains". What should I do?
localhost domains are not supported by default. If you wish to continue supporting them for development you can add them to the list of supported domains for your site key. Go to the [reCAPTCHA Enterprise console](https://console.cloud.google.com/security/recaptcha) or to the [reCAPTCHA console](https://www.google.com/recaptcha/admin), as appropriate, to update your list of supported domains. We advise to use separate keys for development and production, and to only allow localhost on your development site key.

### Version

26.1.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

I expect that the docs are correct and one knows what to put in the mandatory field  host for local development

### Actual behavior

Right now one can assume that the field should be optional and you dont have to fill in anything for development

### How to Reproduce?

its an update.

issue_title: Latency issue after Keycloak version upgrade
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

This was raised on the Keycloak user mailing list: https://groups.google.com/g/keycloak-user/c/6UwBBV8GcY0/m/wXfuxDoQDQAJ

We are currently utilizing Keycloak version 22.0.4 and are in the process of upgrading to version 25.0.6. During our performance testing following the upgrade, we are encountering latency issues, particularly during the authorization phase. We are using the same database as in the previous version.

I am wondering why the below query runs multiple times (20k) after the keycloak version upgrade to 25.0.6.

```
select re1_0.ID from KEYCLOAK_ROLE re1_0 where re1_0.CLIENT_ROLE=0 and re1_0.NAME=? and re1_0.REALM_ID=?
binding parameter (1:NVARCHAR) <- [8bee0f28-9795-4668-8ec8-602e82259aa5]
binding parameter (2:VARCHAR) <- [master]
```

I have conducted validation in Keycloak version 26.1; however, we continue to experience the same query being executed multiple times, and we are not utilizing any custom extensions.

Additionally, I would like to point out that with captured thread dumps, it appears this issue may be originating from here:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/d767c784c9e5bf60e5933162840d536baab8896a/authz/policy/common/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/policy/provider/role/RolePolicyProviderFactory.java#L221

Thanks,
Naresh Kumar

### Version

26.1.0

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

With the fine grained authorizations enabled, Keycloak should still be fast. 

### Actual behavior

There are a lot of unnecessary SQL queries.

### How to Reproduce?

Use an authorization role policy with the ID of the role. 

### Anything else?

@ahus1 is looking into this.

issue_title: Invoking dynamic client registration with lightweight access token results in a 404
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

Using the bootstrap admin client to obtain an access token (lightweight token by default) to dynamically register a client fails with:

```
Request failed: status=403, error=insufficient_scope, description='Forbidden', url=http://localhost:8080/realms/master/clients-registrations/openid-connect
```

Disabling `Always use lightweight access token` without any additional changes works, so this seems to be just down to the fact the dynamic client registration is not "introspecting" lightweight access tokens.

### Version

nightly

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Dynamic client registration supports lightweight access tokens

### Actual behavior

Dynamic client registration results in 403 with lightweight access tokens

### How to Reproduce?

Start Keycloak with `bin/kc.sh start-dev --bootstrap-admin-client-id=test --bootstrap-admin-client-secret=test`

Use the following config for kct:

```
---
default-context: "bootstrap"
store-tokens: false
truststore: {}
issuers:
  test:
    url: "http://localhost:8080/realms/master"
    client-registration-context: "bootstrap"
    contexts:
      bootstrap:
        flow: "client"
        client:
          secret: "test"
          client-id: "test"
```

Then try to create a client:

```
kct config context create --context=myclient --client=myclient --client-secret=secret --flow=client  --create-client --iss test -X
```

With `kct token -d` you can see the token is a lightweight token, and `kct introspect` will show you that the introspected token has access to create clients.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Organization membership for federated users
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

organizations

### Describe the bug

I am using a custom user storage provider, so all my users are federated users.
I can add these federated users to organizations within the console but they are not displayed in the "members" tab of the organization, and the `Organizations.resolveOrganization` method does not return the organization.

### Version

26.1.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

I can manually add a federated user to an organization : 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/658de0df-e046-4b24-b661-d20f1fab7b27)

Then, 
* The members tab of organization page in the console should display organization membership for federated users : 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/84a204a8-9de8-4aa1-8243-469879384e99)

* The organization tab of user page in the console should display organization membership for federated users.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d709c73f-f45b-4cb8-9aba-5ef032274cb3)

* but if I try to re-add the user to organization, we can see that membership is already set : 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/deb71ef0-a571-4ede-a9ab-6fd07aea7432)

* and during authentication, the user is not detected as a member of this organization, so IDP of this organization is not used for example.

### Actual behavior

* The members tab of organization page in the console is not displaying federated users
* The organization tab of user page in the console is not displaying organization of this user
* The resolution of user's organization is wrong when user is authenticating



### How to Reproduce?

* start keycloak with a custom user provider
* create an organization
* add a federated user to this organization with the console
* check if it appears in the members of the organization

### Anything else?

In `Organizations.resolveOrganization` used during authentication flow, organization is resolved from database with `org.keycloak.organization.jpa.JpaOrganizationProvider#getByMember` but the `getGroupsByMember` query executed in this method checks only the `USER_GROUP_MEMBERSHIP` table (ie. for realm users) but not the `FED_USER_GROUP_MEMBERSHIP` (ie. for federated users)

issue_title: RawKeycloakDistribution creates empty directory when copying provider
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

testsuite

### Describe the bug

RawKeycloakDistribution resolves a dependency from Maven, and copies it to the server provider directory. 

`Maven.resolveArtifact` can end up resolving to `<module name>/target/classes` rather than the JAR if the given module has not been packaged. This can for instance happen when running `mvn test` instead of `mvn install|package`.

When this happens it will result in an empty directory being created instead of the actual jar file as `Files.copy` will do that if the source points to a directory.

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/beb20dc425f3e86bfe564537f31f501db9ac6aba/quarkus/tests/junit5/src/main/java/org/keycloak/it/utils/RawKeycloakDistribution.java#L626-L632

This then results in a strange error on startup:

```
Caused by: java.io.FileNotFoundException: /home/runner/work/keycloak/keycloak/tests/base/target/kc-tests/keycloak-999.0.0-SNAPSHOT/lib/../providers/keycloak-tests-custom-providers.jar (Is a directory)
```



### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

`RawKeycloakDistribution.copyProvider` should either copy the JAR successfully, or throw an error. It could either just throw an exception if `Maven.resolveArtifact` doesn't resolve to a file, or it could do something smarter like try to resolve the JAR from the Maven repository, or maybe even package up the provider from `target/classes` into a JAR or something.

### Actual behavior

`RawKeycloakDistribution.copyProvider` returns successfully, but has just created an empty directory instead of copying the actual provider, which leads to a hard to debug error message later.

### How to Reproduce?

Try to resolve a provider in the Maven project that has been built, but not packaged.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Certificate reloading dosen't work for management interface related certificate
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

We run a Keycloak server in k8s environment as a statefulset. 

We set the following env variables to point to the certificate and the key for Keycloak server and management interface 

1. KC_HTTPS_CERTIFICATE_FILE and KC_HTTPS_CERTIFICATE_KEY_FILE for Keycloak server
2. KC_HTTPS_MANAGEMENT_CERTIFICATE_FILE and KC_HTTPS_MANAGEMENT_CERTIFICATE_KEY_FILE for the management interface 
3. KC_HTTPS_CERTIFICATES_RELOAD_PERIOD is set to "5m"

Same set of certificate-key pair is passed for keycloak server and management interface

Our management interface exposes  the metrics and health endpoints.

- When certificate and key is changed, after the specified interval reload happens for the Keycloak server's own keystore certificate and file. This can be verified by running "curl -kv <keycloak-url>" command is run for its service endpoint. 


- Ideal behavior according to the statement present in https://www.keycloak.org/server/enabletls#_certificate_and_key_reloading " is Keycloak will reload the certificates, keys, and keystores specified in https-* options" the same should have happened for the management interface. 
   Do a "curl -kv <keycloak-url>/health" command for its health endpoint. We can see that reloading has not happened and it still points to the initial one used.


### Version

26.1.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

As described in the documentation  https://www.keycloak.org/server/enabletls#_certificate_and_key_reloading , Keycloak will reload the certificates, keys, and keystores specified in https-* options  based on the value we have configured for KC_HTTPS_CERTIFICATES_RELOAD_PERIOD

The reload of certificate related to management interface should have happened  and we should have been able to see this change post the KC_HTTPS_CERTIFICATES_RELOAD_PERIOD value configured.

### Actual behavior

Reloading of certificates related to management interface is not done and it still points to the initial one used.  

### How to Reproduce?

1. Bring up Keycloak server with same certificate-key pair for Keycloak's keystore and for management interface. Set the environment variables for the same along with the KC_HTTPS_CERTIFICATES_RELOAD_PERIOD=5m .

2. Do a curl to keycloak server endpoint and for health endpoint, you will see your certificate being used. 
3. Edit the certificate-key pair used. Wait for the reload interval to pass by.

4. Do a curl to the keycloak-service endpoint, you will be able to see that the certificate has been reloaded. Do a curl to the health endpoint exposed via management interface, you will notice that the certificate still points the initial one that was given 
 

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Organization invitation flow -> changing locale / language does not work
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

organizations

### Describe the bug

When following the organization invite flow to invite someone to join the organization, switching the locale does not work.

It seems like it redirects to the public registration page instead of to the current page (organization invite page) when changing locale / language.

E.g. the link before changing locale is:
- Before changing locale, the link is: `http://localhost/realms/REALMNAME/protocol/openid-connect/registrations?response_type=code&client_id=account&token=TOKEN`
- After changing locale, the link changes to: `http://localhost/realms/REALMNAME/login-actions/registration?client_id=account&tab_id=TABID&client_data=TOKEN&kc_locale=nl`



### Version

nightly

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

It will switch the current page to a different locale

### Actual behavior

It shows an error `Registration not allowed` that is translated based on the locale. 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4be5a190-fa9c-48e8-b3b1-4c21e80fd5fb)

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create or update a realm to have the following:

- Localization is enabled
- Localization has 2 or more locales configured/enabled
- Disable public user registration (uncheck `User registration` in realm settings)
- Enable organizations
- Create at least one organization

2. Go to organizations -> go to the organization -> press invite member -> invite someone by their email address
3. Open the invitation email and click the link
4. Result: it will render the form to enter your details, and have a locale dropdown
5. On the page select a different locale/language

### Anything else?

- Before changing locale, the link is: `http://localhost/realms/REALMNAME/protocol/openid-connect/registrations?response_type=code&client_id=account&token=TOKEN`
- After changing locale, the link changes to: `http://localhost/realms/REALMNAME/login-actions/registration?client_id=account&tab_id=TABID&client_data=TOKEN&kc_locale=nl`

issue_title: Wrong organization claim assignment in JWT access token
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

organizations

### Describe the bug

User (that email domain matches domain already used in any organizations) without any organization assigned gets organization claim in access token witch is in my opinion very very unwanted situation. 
Issue occurs when we create user with email domain that is assigned to organization. Then even so user is not a part of the organization gets organization claim in access token. (user has no organization and organization has no users).
Wrong claim is assigned only using client with real application - evaluation tool for that particular client works correctly.

### Version

nightly

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

User with no organization has no organization claim - even if its email's domain name is the same as one used in existing organization.

### Actual behavior

User with no organization gets organization token claim with real organization data.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create realm
2. Enable organizations
3. Set email as username
4. Modify client scope "**organization**" in client scopes:
   1) Set as default
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a3cadef2-8c26-427d-81ae-3d95d2b5f90f)

   2) Modify default mapper 'organizations' in client scope 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/edf109ef-2a51-4972-a318-cd98fd77f099)

       Before:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/47f41840-0b1b-4a2c-b684-f344c6e95906)

       After:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4f2497df-7adf-4526-b560-509bfbee5f59)

5. Create client that will be used by real application.
6. Ensure that scope organization is default 
7. Create organizations

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/254a9590-38b8-40d2-8b2a-bb7363b976d6)

9. Create user with mail that is used by one of organizations (do not assign to any organization)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e3305c8f-51b0-4a99-a53d-9b953b0c8b89)
Org also has no users:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/84066057-d438-41c2-abf0-4a5097e6327a)
11. Log in to application
12. Token contains organization based on domain while user is not assigned to any domain

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7055aee3-7b56-4e62-bb86-230916365893)



14. Evaluation tool works correctly

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fb8ce147-b24c-4ced-9182-46f102b50610)

(wrote all steps I've done)



### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Client scopes evaluate function shows sub claim in access token even if "basic" client scope is not selected
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

With one of recent Keycloak updated the "sub" claim is no longer added by default to the access token. It has been reworked into a mapper and a client scope "basic". This is working as designed for real clients but if you go to Clients->Client scopes->Evaluate the sub claim is always present in the access token even if "basic" client scope is not set to Default.



### Version

26.0.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

I expect that the access token in admin ui evaluate function is identical to an access token retrieved by a real client, especially only containing the "sub" claim if added by a mapper or via the "basic" client scope.

### Actual behavior

The "sub" claim is always displayed in the access token.

### How to Reproduce?

Just create a simple client with default and click on evaluate in the client scopes, take a look at the access token. It contains the "sub" claim though not mapped.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Change default value for force-login option in reset-credential-email
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

Follow-up of #36844. After talking with the team we decided to change the default value for the new `force-login` option in the `reset-credential-email` authenticator. This should be secure by default. Two possible solutions:

* Change the default to true to always login again.
* Add a third option similar to `only-federated` that only force the login for federated users and make it default. This way the behavior is only changed for federated users and internal DB users work in the same way.

N/A
